article.description: Al Faux scored 20 points including a 15-foot jumper with 19 seconds remaining that lifted San Diego State to a 70-69 win over Wyoming in the Mountain West Conference tournament semifinals Friday.
article.headline: Late Faux jumper lifts Aztecs to victory
article.linkText: Aztecs send MWC's top seed back to Wyoming on bubble
article.story:
LAS VEGAS (AP) -- Al Faux scored 20 points, including a 15-foot
jumper with 19 seconds remaining that lifted San Diego State to a
70-69 win over Wyoming in the Mountain West Conference tournament
semifinals Friday.

	   The Aztecs (20-11) advanced to just their third Division I
conference championship game and first since the 1985 Western
Athletic Conference tournament. They will play the winner of the
UNLV-Utah matchup late Friday.

	   Marcus Bailey missed a jumper with 4 seconds left for Wyoming,
and Aerick Sanders grabbed the rebound for the Aztecs and was
fouled with 1.3 seconds left. He missed both free throws, but
Wyoming couldn't get off another shot.

	   Bailey finished with a season-high 31 points for the Cowboys
(21-8).

	   "Marc took us as far as he could take us, and it just wasn't
quite enough tonight," Wyoming coach Steve McClain said.

	   Wyoming will have to wait until Sunday afternoon to see if it is
chosen to play in the NCAA Tournament. The Cowboys are sure to get
into the NIT if they are passed over.

	   "Come Sunday we're going to play more basketball and that's
what we've established in this program that come March we're going
to keep playing basketball, and that's all I care about," McClain
said.

	   But he said it would be disappointing for the NCAA to overlook
Wyoming's regular-season outright Mountain West Conference
championship and leave the Cowboys out of the tournament.

	   Wyoming trailed most of the game against SDSU, taking the lead
at 59-58 on David Rottinghaus' two free throws with 7:28 remaining.

	   Sanders followed a missed shot with a layup to pull the Aztecs
within 67-66 with 2:07 remaining and Holcomb scored off a Faux
assist 37 seconds later to give the Aztecs a 68-67 lead. Bailey
then sank two free throws for a 69-68 Wyoming advantage, setting up
Faux's heroics.

	   Randy Holcomb scored 12 of his 19 points in the first half to
lead the Aztecs to a 37-32 halftime advantage. Holcomb finished
with 19 points and Sanders 10 for San Diego State.
